notes: Overnight fresh-boot matrix v3 (stock-first: characterize the 8/8 winner before any code change) (post-EXO-research order). Every trial: dual fresh boot -> env apply -> optional mlx variant install + kernel gates -> boot -> wait_ready -> coherence gate -> 16k prefill probe -> soak -> record -> dual reboot. Baseline: 2026-07-05 evening soak (replication config, iogpu untuned, FAST_SYNCH=1 forced by launch_cluster) = rounds at 26.34/26.17/25.99/25.83/~26 t/s decode. Goal criteria: decode 26-34, prefill high-300s @16k, caching/SSD features ON in any candidate final config. Trials 0-2 attack the mlx#3142 fence-stall theory three ways; 8 is diagnostic-only.

trials.name: replication-repeat (variance anchor)
trials.why: Same config as the 2026-07-05 evening soak on a fresh boot — reproducibility/variance estimate for everything else.
trials.note: FALSE WEDGE — orchestrator infra bug, NOT an RDMA failure. wait_ready raced past warmup (checked for m3_warmup before it started), soak hit cold server, died rc=1 in seconds (no jsonl = never ran a round). Verdict logic conflated rc!=0 with wedge. No cluster fault occurred.

trials.name: agent-traffic pattern (pristine env)
trials.why: The OTHER wedge regime: the 2026-07-05 morning wedge was a plain ~127-token decode. Multi-turn tool-call sessions, short bursty decodes, growing context, cache reuse/eviction — Codex/Claude-Code-shaped load the goal actually serves.
trials.note: FALSE WEDGE — same infra bug as trial 0, second loop iteration.

trials.name: FAST_SYNCH=0 on stock (mlx#3142 fence-stall, cheapest fix)
trials.why: launch_cluster.sh forces FAST_SYNCH=1; on STOCK mlx that exposes wontfix mlx#3142 (GPU fence_wait spin -> rank never sends -> peer polls forever = our silent-wedge signature). EXO only survives =1 via fork fence patches. All caching features stay ON.
trials.note: KILLED BY OPERATOR mid-boot while breaking the reboot loop. No verdict. Config never soaked.

trials.name: iogpu tuned (rank0 260096 / rank1 122880)
trials.why: Fidelity to original interactive-boot clean runs (rank0) + first-ever rank1 tune (sudo -n always failed silently; rank1 in-process wired target 120GB exceeds ~96GB default OS cap). Prime suspect for prefill (rank1 unwires/rewires 84GB per 4096-token chunk) and the -0.5%/round decode drift.

trials.name: back-to-back rounds (gap=0, idle-gap hypothesis)
trials.why: Historical wedges clustered ~round 3 with 30s idle gaps. Back-to-back surviving longer implicates idle-time link power-state transitions rather than cumulative traffic.

trials.name: ring backend control
trials.why: Expected 8/8 at ~27-31 t/s. Anchors machine/model/server health overnight + speed comparison; ring wedging too would exonerate jaccl.

trials.name: background OFF isolation (DIAGNOSTIC ONLY — not a candidate final)
trials.why: Isolation re-run on a FRESH link (the churned-link isolation result may be void). Tests background-ON-protective theory. Goal requires caching/SSD ON in the final config, so this is evidence-gathering only.

trials.name: EXO fork build (fence patches + FAST_SYNCH=1)
trials.why: rltakashige/mlx-jaccl-fix-small-recv@address-rdma-gpu-locks — the only config publicly demonstrated running TP MoE at scale over jaccl (DSB-SY fence coherence, 2s command-buffer pacing under the ~5s IOGPU watchdog, own all_reduce race fix). FAST_SYNCH stays 1 (their combo).
trials.note: deferred 2026-07-05 eve: characterize the winning stock config first (Jonathan's call); revisit with tonight's data

trials.name: upstream main build (post-0.31.2 jaccl fixes)
trials.why: main-only fixes absent from 0.31.2: #3451 all_reduce race, #3459 jaccl barrier, #3654 recv deadlock. #3142 remains wontfix upstream, so this may still stall — a wedge here + clean EXO-fork trial = fence patches are the difference (upstreamable finding).
trials.note: deferred 2026-07-05 eve: characterize the winning stock config first (Jonathan's call); revisit with tonight's data
